ref: They told him, ‚ÄúHe will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
text: Nfa nf…õ b…î…ît…îk…î nw…î al…õ, ‚ÄúBetlehem okpoo di Yudea kas…î suoto babalofo nw…î. Diekye nimmle okle di Yaa …îlaa bu…õt…õt…õ l…õkpana ni al…õ,
translated: And it happened in those days that a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be registered. This census first took place while Quirinius was governing Syria.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: ‚ÄúBitanyu bil…õ atoko …îky…õ ole l…õy…î l…õwako kanyi ka mmle. Oowo! Ninwako atoko …îky…õ ole. Kaf…î…î kapamii l…õwako.
translated: The dog is very happy to see its owner coming home today. Oh! The dog is very happy to see its owner. The owner is also very happy to see the dog.

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: Ninf…õ aat…îk…î ma al…õ, ‚ÄúKufiofa batuot…õ ku Farisi batii ninle mba b…õ…õta osuku al…õ baadiki ni Mose kufiofa nwu nkpo kas…î.
translated: And they said to him, "Why do the Pharisees and the disciples of John fast often, but your disciples do not fast?"

ref: A man came to Jesus and asked, ‚ÄúTeacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?‚Äù
text: Dii ninwii osu…ît…î onwii lawa Yesu …îky…õ aabakaal…õ nw…î al…õ, ‚ÄúSaatuot…õ, l…õm…õnsaa biene kabla fi…õ di Yaa kat…õ…õ nkpa be ninnaa ni kaloo?‚Äù
translated: Why do you call me Lord, Lord, and do not do the things that I say?

ref: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
text: ‚ÄúOtii lele ninan-y…õ mi kamaa yi, ni …õ…õtaka ay…õ mi di suoto. Otii lele ninankyakaakoe busiisa, ni …îsamiisa, …î…îsaamiisa.
translated: When I went to the farm, I saw a python, and it was very big. When I went to the farm again, I saw a big snake, a small snake, and a very small snake.

ref: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.‚Äù The Final Judgment
text: Kp…õmblat…õ w…î mmle diesinl…õ ata l…õsal…õsaa …îblako, nkpo oso biy…î nw…î bifuki binyu kaayi di ditunu nwu nkpo kam…õ. Nfa abawi kaku anwee any…õ…õ n…õ. Otii Obi Nwu Nkpo Nimakanko Batii Bamuu Kaayi.
translated: The chief of the town went to the farm with his workers to work, but they did not see any animal or bird there. He then called his people to come and see. It is the god of the land who has taken all the animals and birds away.

ref: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
text: ‚ÄúNkpo oso nse k…în…õ…õ l…õf…î, ee k…îkpaa l…õf…î mata al…õ fambla okpile yi, faabudi k≈© fayu. Dil…õ dilenke al…õ faay…î k…în…õ…õ kunwii ee k…îkpaa kunwii fambuo di nkpa nwu kam…õ, al…õ faaw…õ nn…õ…õ nny…î ee nkpaa nny…î, man-y…î f…î manfuki mankpee di kaw…î…î …ît…î w…î ninnaa ni kaloo kam…õ.
translated: When you are going to the farm, take your farm tools and seeds, and go to the place where you will farm. Clear the land and make it ready for farming. If you have yams, take them to the farm and plant them. If you have rice, take it to the farm and plant it. When you are farming, be careful not to waste the seeds, and make sure you plant them well so that they can grow well.

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: ‚ÄúNinf…õ aay…î koto fututu ninle ni dii k…îkp…õnto kunwii kunwii aata batii ba niawa ni k…ît…î…îf…î abombo an…î…î kam…õ n…õ.
translated: The woman went to the farm and saw that the yam had grown very well, so she was very happy and started to sing.

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: ‚ÄúBinu lo! Obe onwii alesaa kubi sant…õ onwii laky…õ kofe aalaasa alesaa kubi.
translated: The child has died! The mother is crying, the father is also crying, the child has died, the mother is crying, the father is also crying.

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, ‚ÄúHe will be called a Nazarene.‚Äù
text: Obe w…î aalaabuo ni nfa, aasifi aalaasi…õ di okpoo w…î b…î…îl…õ…õ ni Nasaret. Nfa l…õma …îlaasi…õ lata al…õ, …îlaa w…î di Yaa …îlaa babu…õt…õt…õ labu…õ baat…õ ni al…õ, ‚ÄúBatii mal…õ…õ nw…î ni Nasaret otii yi,‚Äù diawa kaanya ninkpo.
translated: And it happened in those days that a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be registered. This registration first took place when Quirinius was governing Syria. And all went to be registered, everyone to his own city. Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the town of Nazareth, into Judea, to the city of David, which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and lineage of David, to be registered with Mary, his betrothed wife, who was with child.
